rulings.Case Title: 1200 CALIFORNIA STREET CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. PAIGE STRUCTURAL GLASS CO., A CORPORATION DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 21, 2011, line 4, PLAINTIFF 1200 CALIFORNIA STREET CORPORATION, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FIRST, PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO ASSERT A BREACH OF IMPLIED WARRANTY CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT. CC SECTION 1368.3; WINDHAM AT CARMEL MOUNTAIN RANCH ASSOC. V. SUP. CRT. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 1162, 1173. SECOND, DEFENDANT HAS COMPLIED WITH CRC 3.1354(b)(4) IN THAT THEY HAVE STATED GROUNDS FOR THEIR OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE IN THEIR OPPOSITION. THE OBJECTIONS, HOWEVER, ARE OVERRULED. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF NEGATING ANY TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO A NECESSARY ELEMENT OF IMPLIED WARRANTY, REASONABLENESS OF WORKMANSHIP HAS NOT BEEN ESTABLISHED. APPLY THE STANDARD PROFERRED BY PLAINTIFF (THE "NON-CHROME EQUIVALENT" STANDARD), PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET THE STANDARD. THE INDUSTRY STANDARD SET FORTH BY PLAINTIFF REQUIRES CONTRACTORS TO USE EITHER A CHROME CONVERSION OR A NON-CHROME EQUIVALENT. PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT STATES THAT THE EXISTENCE OF A NON-CHROME EQUIVALENT WAS "INCONCLUSIVE". =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LEW JENKINS VS. THE PROPERTIES SCIENCES GROUP, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Portions Of Defts Answer To First Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing for Thursday, July 21, 2011, Line 6. PLAINTIFFS LEW JENKINS, LEW JENKINS, LINDA JENKINS AND JACK FRESKOS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Portions Of Defendants Answer To First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to Defenses 3 (contrib. negl.), 5 (other's comparative fault) and 23 (assumption of risk) is Granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show plaintiff's alleged irrational conduct. Plaintiff's motion as to Defense 7 (no reasonalbe reliance) is granted without leave to amend as this is not an affirmative defense but raises an element of Plaintiff's Prima Facie case on the claim of alleged misrepresentation. Defendant's denial of the allegations in the complaint put Plaintiff's reasonable reliance at issue. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CALVIN B. GRIGSBY et al VS. LUANN LABEDZ, PUBLISHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 21, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANTS CREATIVE LOAFING ATLANTA, CHANTE LAGON, THOMAS WHEATLEY MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) IS GRANTED. FIRST THIS CASE IMPLICATES BOTH CCP SECTION 425.16(e)(2) and (e)(4). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DEFAMATION AND INVASION OF PRIVACY BASED SOLELY ON AN ARTICLE THAT WAS PUBLISHED BY THE SECOND MOST BROADLY DISTRIBUTED NEWSPAPER IN GEORGIA. THE ARTICLE WAS WRITTEN TO REPORT ON THE ATLANTA AIRPORT BOND DEAL WHICH CLEARLY IS A MATTER OF PUBLIC INTEREST. ACCORDINGLY, THE COMPLAINT AND CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. SECOND, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN ANY PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. FIRST, THE ARTICLE IS SUBSTANTIALLY TRUE AND THEREFORE PRIVILEGED UNDER THE CONSTITUTION. RINGER ASSOC. V. MARYLAND CASUALTY CO. (2008) 80 CAL.APP.4TH 1165, 1180. SECOND, THE ARTICLE IS PROTECTED BY STATUTE AS A FAIR AND TRUE REPORT OF OFFICIAL PROCEEDINGS UNDER CCP SECTION 47(d). SUCH REPORTS MUST MERELY REFLECT THE "GIST" OF THE OFFICIAL PROCEEDING AND RECORDS. JENNINGS V. TELEGRAM-TRIBUNE CO. (1985) 164 CAL.APP.3D 119, 125. MOREOVER PLAINTIFFS DO NOT DISPUTE THAT A STATEMENT IS ABSOLUTELY PROTECTED AS A FAIR REPORT UNDER SECTION 47 OR THE FIRST AMENDMENT IF THE STATEMENT CAPTURES THE GIST AND STING OF THE OFFICIAL REPORTS OR PROCEEDINGS, EVEN IF THERE ARE SLIGHT INACCURACIES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: OLGA K., A MINOR BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN AD VS. SECURITY INDUSTRY SPECIALISTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 21, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT ALLIEDBARTON SECURITY SERVICES LP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action for negligence is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that Defendant had a duty of care to plaintiff (e.g. duty to comply with Penal Code §847(a)). Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action for negligent supervision is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to support how Defendant was negligent in supervising its own guard. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action for premises liability is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that Defendant was aware that it created a dangerous condition when it¿s employee turned custody of Plaintiff over to Yngojowang. Defendant¿s Demurrer is to the 11th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead further facts to support its claim that Defendant owed a duty of care to P and that P suffered serious emotional distress. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Certification Of Facts To Initiate Contempt Proceedings Under Gvt. Code Section 11455.20(A)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 21, 2011, line 11, PETITIONER WILLIAM CHOW'S Notice Of Petition For Certification Of Facts To Initiate Contempt Proceedings Under Gvt. Code Section 11455.20(A). PETITION TO INITIATE CONTEMPT IS DENIED. THE PURPOSE OF A SECTION 11525 CONTEMPT PROCEEDING IS TO MAINTAIN THE DIGNITY OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE TRIBUNAL AND TO ENFORCE ITS ORDERS AND CAN ONLY BE BROUGHT BY THE AGENCY, NOT A PARTY TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING. PARRIS V. ZOLLIN (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 839. =(302/LMG)
